verdict_rationale: All 4 BLOCKING gates pass on all 7 public pages (semantic_audit, prefers_reduced_motion, canonical_check, cls_hygiene). Non-blocking warnings: (1) /elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf referenced from 12 places across 7 pages but file does not exist — already flagged by Agent 5+6 as pending_post_launch; (2) Formspree action URL is literal placeholder 'PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID' — pre-launch swap required; (3) one wrapper <section> in contact.html (line 302) has no aria-labelledby — it is a layout-only wrapper whose children carry their own aria-labelledby, so screen-reader outline is intact; (4) .nav-toggle is 40x40 and .btn padding renders ~38px tall — both below the WCAG 2.5.5 enhanced 44x44 target, though above the WCAG 2.5.5 Level AA fallback of 24x24. Recorded as low-severity findings. No critical or serious axe-equivalent violations.

stats.note_low_internal_links: Only 2 internal links in <main> (footer adds more). Borderline of seo-no-internal-links threshold (≥3). Counted as info, not a violation, because footer links satisfy crawler traversal.

violations.evidence: <section style="padding-top:0;"> at contact.html:302 — wrapper around owner-card aside + contact-form. Children (#owner-card, the form) each carry their own aria-labelledby, so screen-reader outline is intact, but the wrapper itself is unlabelled.
violations.fix: Add aria-labelledby that references contact-form-heading (or aria-label="Contact form layout"), or convert the wrapper to a <div> since it carries no landmark semantics.

prefers_reduced_motion.note: Animations present (scroll-reveal IntersectionObserver in assets/js/main.js + CSS transitions on buttons/cards). Both guards in place: (a) assets/css/main.css:852 has @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) sweeping *,*::before,*::after to 0.001ms duration + iteration 1 + scroll-behavior:auto + .feature-card:hover/.btn:hover transform:none. (b) assets/js/main.js:13 short-circuits via window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches.
Same global guard as home (shared assets/css/main.css + assets/js/main.js).
Same global guard as home. 404.html is mostly static — minimal animation surface.

cls_hygiene.note: 2 (nav brand-mark 104x36, footer brand-mark 116x40). Both have explicit width + height + alt. No loading= attribute on either; nav image is above-fold (correctly not lazy), footer image is below-fold and could carry loading="lazy" but browser default eager-load of a 591-byte logo is negligible. Recorded as info only.
Same image pattern as home — 2 brand-mark logos, both have explicit width/height/alt.
3 — nav brand-mark, parent-band 180x62 (above fold mid-page), footer brand-mark. All have explicit width/height/alt.

axe_summary.scan_method: mental-axe-equivalent via Python regex audit + manual rule walkthrough (no live axe-core runner — site not yet deployed and there is no headless-browser harness in this project)
minor_findings: contact.html:302 — wrapper <section> without aria-labelledby (low; child landmarks carry the outline)
pending_post_launch: Run https://www.deque.com/axe/devtools/ DevTools extension on the deployed URL to confirm 0 critical/serious
Run Lighthouse a11y on the deployed URL to confirm score ≥95

iphone_13_390x844.notes: Viewport meta present on every page. Hamburger (.nav-toggle) shows at <920px, well above the 768px iPhone width. Hamburger panel (.mobile-nav) is position:fixed below the 64px nav. Mobile nav toggle button is 40x40 — below WCAG 2.5.5 enhanced 44x44 (LOW severity finding, see mobile_findings[]). No CSS rule sets a fixed width >360px outside max-width containers, so no horizontal-scroll risk at 360px. Container padding 24px reduces gracefully. Buttons render ~38px tall (padding 12px 22px + 0.85rem font) — below 44px enhanced target but above 24px Level AA fallback.
iphone_13_390x844.tested_via: CSS audit + main.js inspection (no live device test in this archetype build)

pixel_7_412x915.notes: Same mobile breakpoint handling as iPhone 13. Pixel-specific concerns (Chrome address bar viewport) not applicable to static informational site without 100vh hero.

ipad_768x1024.notes: 768px portrait still falls below the 920px hamburger breakpoint, so iPad portrait gets the hamburger menu. iPad landscape (1024px) gets the desktop primary-nav. Grid breakpoints (640/768/1024) handle column counts gracefully on both orientations. No bleed.

mobile_findings.issue: 40x40px — below WCAG 2.5.5 enhanced (44x44) but above Level AA (24x24).
mobile_findings.fix: Bump width/height from 40px to 44px, or add padding:2px to expand hit area.

mobile_findings.issue: padding:12px 22px + font-size 0.85rem renders ~38-40px tall. Below 44px enhanced target.
mobile_findings.fix: Bump padding-block from 12px to 14px (or min-height:44px) on .btn.

pending_post_launch: Real iPhone test of hamburger open/close (qa-mobile-not-tested risk).
Real Android Chrome test of contact form submit (Formspree happy path) once PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID is swapped.

seo_sanity.og_tags_per_page: complete (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type on every page)
seo_sanity.jsonld_validator_script: scripts/validate-jsonld.py — all 20 blocks across 7 pages parse via json.loads (verified by Agent 6 and re-verified by Agent 7 inline regex+json.loads)
seo_sanity.robots_txt_status: Allow: / + Disallow: /admin/ + Disallow: /colors.html + Sitemap reference. Production-safe (no Disallow: /).
seo_sanity.canonical_base_url_consistency: pass — sitemap.xml, all 7 <link rel=canonical>, robots.txt Sitemap directive all use https://derrick-pixel.github.io/merchandising/ as the base. No origin disagreement.

broken_internal_links.agent_flag: Already flagged by Agent 5 (copy.json.notes_for_human) and Agent 6 (assets-manifest.json.pending_post_launch). Deck PDF is to be produced post-launch by a separate sales workflow — site references the canonical URL ahead of asset existence as a deliberate trade-off.

link_integrity.external_target_blank_with_rel_noopener: pass — no anchor uses target="_blank" without rel set elsewhere (none found at all in the 7 pages); external footer links open in the same tab.
link_integrity.verdict: warning — 12 broken refs to one missing asset, pre-existing/expected, not a QA blocker.

form_integrity.formspree_action_status: PLACEHOLDER — pre-launch swap required before going live (see pitfall_proposals[].si-formspree-placeholder-launch-blocker)
form_integrity.pdpa_consent_label_pattern: implicit (input wrapped inside <label class=form-checkbox>) — WAI-ARIA valid
form_integrity.aria_error_notes: Form fields use aria-required via the HTML5 required attribute. No aria-invalid/aria-describedby for inline error messages — current pattern is server-side validation via Formspree redirect. If client-side validation is added post-launch, wire aria-invalid + aria-describedby to error span ids.

performance.status: Not run — site not yet deployed to live URL. Lighthouse pending post-launch. Static-vanilla-HTML profile (no JS framework, two small CSS files totalling ~46KB, one ~7KB JS file, hand-tuned 57KB og-image.jpg) suggests Performance ≥90, Accessibility ≥95, SEO ≥95 — verify on deployed URL.

pitfall_proposals.story: Contact form on /contact.html ships with action="https://formspree.io/f/PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID" — a literal placeholder. If the site is deployed before this is swapped to the real Formspree form ID, every submission silently 404s at Formspree's edge and the lead is lost. Agent 5+6 added it as a TODO in copy.json.notes_for_human, but Agent 7 noticed there's no pre-launch checklist item in the static-informational archetype that enforces a swap-before-deploy gate.
pitfall_proposals.fix: (a) Agent 6 emits a /data/launch-checklist.json file listing every placeholder string still in the codebase (regex: PLACEHOLDER_, TODO:, <FIXME>, FORM_ID). (b) Agent 7 grep-fails the QA gate if any such placeholder remains AND brief.json.deploy_target indicates the site is going to a production URL (not just GitHub Pages preview). (c) For preview/dev builds, placeholders are allowed but the page shows a yellow banner 'Form in preview mode — submissions not delivered'.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: Placeholder = silent failure. Make placeholders LOUD before launch.

pitfall_proposals.story: /elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf is referenced 12 times across 7 pages (footer link, contact deck-download band, hero CTA on some pages) before the asset exists. The deck is to be produced post-launch by a separate sales workflow. Agent 5 + Agent 6 both noted this as pending, but the broken link still ships with the site — clicking 'Download Deck' returns a 404. The current pattern leaves it to the team to remember to remove or replace the link.
pitfall_proposals.source_citation: scripts/validate-jsonld.py-adjacent + manual grep of all 7 HTML pages — 12 occurrences
pitfall_proposals.fix: Static-informational archetype should add a /data/asset-readiness.json field: any asset referenced from copy.json or sitemap.json that hasn't been produced yet either (a) gets gated behind a 'data-asset-pending' attribute that Agent 4 renders as a disabled-looking link or 'Coming soon' badge, or (b) Agent 6 emits the link with rel="nofollow" + a /coming-soon.html target, or (c) Agent 7 hard-fails the link-integrity check unless the broken asset is explicitly listed in assets-manifest.json.pending_post_launch[]. Current site uses option (c) implicitly — but it should be formalised, not relied on as folklore.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: If you reference an asset that doesn't exist yet, mark the reference, don't hide it.

pitfall_proposals.story: Static-informational CLAUDE.md spec (Section 6, Buttons) says padding: 12px 24px + font-size 0.78rem — which renders ~38px tall. .nav-toggle in the playbook is 20x20 hamburger spans inside a parent button (this project bumped it to 40x40, still below 44x44). Both are below the WCAG 2.5.5 Level AAA enhanced touch target of 44x44 (passing the Level AA 24x24 fallback). Vestibular users + users with motor impairment + thumb-bowling buyers on a 6.1" iPhone all hit-test smaller buttons less reliably. Three sites in a row (Elitez Pulse, ELIX EOR, Elitez Merchandising) have shipped with this pattern.
pitfall_proposals.fix: Static-informational CLAUDE.md should bump the .btn template padding to padding: 14px 24px (yields ~44px tall) and the .nav-toggle to width: 44px; height: 44px (or add display:flex + min-width:44px to the parent). Cost: 2 px per side, no design impact at desktop. Mobile finger ergonomics noticeably improve.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: WCAG 44x44 is a thumb size, not a checkbox. Bake it into the template.

pitfall_proposals.story: Agent 4 sometimes inserts a layout-only <section> wrapper around two sibling components that each carry their own aria-labelledby (e.g., owner-card aside + contact-form in contact.html:302). The wrapper itself is unlabelled, which trips a semantic-html-audit rule. The fix is either (a) convert the wrapper to <div> (no landmark semantics needed) or (b) add aria-labelledby to the wrapper. Agent 4 had no rule saying 'don't use <section> for pure layout' — and the wrapper here actually only exists to set padding-top:0 on a column grid. A <div> would have been correct.
pitfall_proposals.fix: Add to static-informational/agents.md Agent 4 deliverable checklist: 'A <section> is a landmark. If you're wrapping for padding/grid only, use <div>. If you're wrapping for semantic grouping, add aria-labelledby pointing to the wrapper's heading id.' Agent 7 semantic_audit can grep for `<section[^>]*>` without aria-labelledby/aria-label and surface them as low-severity warnings.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: <section> is a contract. Use <div> for layout, <section> for landmarks.

pitfall_proposals.story: Every page in this 7-page site has the same '/elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf' CTA appearing twice (footer + at least one in-page band) — 12 references in total. When the deck wasn't produced yet, the broken-link sprawl multiplied. Agent 3 (Information Architect) treats CTAs as page-local decisions; it doesn't budget how many times the SAME CTA appears across the site. The risk: when the target asset breaks or moves, you have N pages to update instead of one.
pitfall_proposals.source_citation: All 7 HTML files referencing /elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf — 12 total occurrences
pitfall_proposals.fix: Agent 3 emits a /data/sitemap.json.shared_ctas[] array: a CTA that appears on >1 page is hoisted here with id, label, href, allowed_pages. Agent 4 reads this and renders shared CTAs via a template binding (data-shared-cta="deck-download") so href can be updated in one place. Agent 6's link-integrity check uses the same source of truth.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: A site-wide CTA is a site-wide contract. Lift it out of per-page copy.

deferred_for_human_followup: Run https://search.google.com/test/rich-results on / and /contact.html and /emr-intel.html post-deploy to verify JSON-LD eligibility (Organization + WebSite + BreadcrumbList + FAQPage).
Run https://validator.schema.org/ on the same three URLs.
Run https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator on / to verify summary_large_image card.
Real iPhone (Safari) + real Android (Chrome) test of hamburger open/close — qa-mobile-not-tested risk noted by pitfalls.md.
Lighthouse run on deployed URL — record Performance / Accessibility / SEO scores. Targets: Performance ≥85, Accessibility ≥95, SEO ≥90.
Swap PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID in contact.html for the real Formspree form ID, then submit a test message to verify end-to-end (formspree → derrick@elitez.asia or jack.wang@elitez.asia).
Produce /elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf (sales workflow) and verify all 12 references resolve.
Once /elitez-merchandising-deck.pdf is produced, re-run the broken-internal-link grep to confirm zero broken refs.
things_i_did_not_run: Live axe-core scan — site not deployed; no headless-browser harness in this archetype. Mental-axe-equivalent applied via Python regex audit instead.
Touch tests on a real device — environment is a macOS workstation.
Real Formspree submission test — PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID would 404.
